sections.paragraph:
- Original: πρόσκομμα

- Transliteration: Proskomma

- Phonetic: pros'-kom-mah

- Definition:

1. a stumbling block

a. an obstacle in the way which if one strikes his foot against he stumbles or falls

b. that over which a soul stumbles i.e. by which is caused to sin

- Origin: from G4350

- TDNT entry: 18:25,9

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Neuter

- Strong's: From G4350; a stub that is (figuratively) occasion of apostasy: - offence stumbling (-block6[-stone]).
